lastMessage.text: You're going to absolutely crush your presentation. I believe in you! 🚀

messages.text: Hey Vedaant! I saw your post about ConnectFBLA in the Mobile App Dev channel. What framework are you using for the chat system?
Hey Maya! I'm using Zustand for state management — it handles the chat state beautifully. FlatList with inverted prop for the message scroll.
Smart! I used Redux last year but Zustand is so much cleaner. Did you add image picking for attachments? That's a great wow factor.
Yes! Using expo-image-picker. Also added emoji reactions, read receipts, typing indicators, and resource sharing into chat. Trying to make it feel like a real messaging app.
That's incredible for an FBLA competition! Judges are going to lose their minds. Make sure you have a clean 7-minute script that hits every rubric point.
That's my plan. Starting with the planning doc, then MVVM architecture, then demo each feature systematically. 4 minutes for the demo should be enough.
Perfect structure. One tip: when you demo validation, intentionally trigger both error types (syntactic and semantic) live in front of judges. They love seeing it work.
Great tip! I'll add that to my demo script. Really appreciate the help Maya — you're the best.
Good luck tomorrow! Text me after your presentation. I want to hear how it goes.
Will do! Good luck to you too! What time is your slot?
11:45 AM in Room C-6. Back to back with lunch so I'm going last before the break. Wish me luck!
You're going to absolutely crush your presentation. I believe in you! 🚀

lastMessage.text: Meet you at the elevator at 8:15. Let's go get that trophy 🏆

messages.text: Vedaant! Quick question — are you using React Navigation v6 or v7? I hit a weird issue with nested navigators on v7.
Using v6! Stayed on v6 for stability — v7 still has some rough edges. What's the issue you're seeing?
Tab navigator inside a stack navigator. The headerShown prop is being ignored in some cases. Classic react nav issue.
Try setting headerShown: false on the tab screen inside the parent stack. That usually fixes the nested header issue.
That fixed it! Thanks man. We CS kids have to stick together at these competitions 😄
Absolutely! What time is your presentation tomorrow?
9:30 AM. Early slot — actually I prefer it. Get it done and then I can relax and cheer on everyone else. You're at 10:30 right?
Yeah 10:30 in C-4. Nervous but ready. The app is really polished — I just hope the judges appreciate the technical depth.
Judges for Mobile App Dev are usually software engineers or CS teachers. They'll appreciate the architecture and clean code 100%.
Meet you at the elevator at 8:15. Let's go get that trophy 🏆

lastMessage.text: I'm so proud of what you built. ConnectFBLA is going to win. See you at breakfast! ☀️

messages.text: Hey Vedaant, as chapter VP I just wanted to say — the way you've represented us in Mobile App Dev this year has been amazing. The work ethic you've shown is inspiring.
That genuinely means so much, Sophia. FBLA and Cherry Creek are such a big part of why I love building things. I built ConnectFBLA FOR US.
That's exactly why it's going to win. The authenticity comes through in every feature. You can tell it was built by someone who actually uses FBLA.
I hope the judges see it that way! I'll show them the real-time chat with seed messages from our actual District 12 conversations. That should make it real.
That's brilliant honestly. The meta quality of an FBLA app with FBLA members talking about the FBLA competition inside the app itself 😄
I'm so proud of what you built. ConnectFBLA is going to win. See you at breakfast! ☀️

lastMessage.text: Proud of you man. Go show them what we're made of!

messages.text: Vedaant — can I see a demo of your app before State? I want to know what I'm competing against (in a friendly way lol)
Ha! Sure! Different events though — you're Business Plan, I'm Mobile App. No conflict! Come by room 204 after school tomorrow.
Did the demo — this app is legit. The chat feature alone is better than some production apps I've used. You're going to place top 3 for sure.
That's the goal! Your business plan sounds equally impressive from what you shared in the channel. The sustainability angle is really smart.
Thanks! Okay let's both win tomorrow and go to NLC Atlanta together. Deal?
DEAL. Atlanta 2026, let's go! 🏆✈️
Proud of you man. Go show them what we're made of!
